title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR MIXED ZAATAR (Near East) 1 CXS 341R-2020

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR MIXED ZAATAR (Near East) 1 CXS 341R-2020
sections.summary: Document heading and adoption note: Regional Standard for Mixed Zaatar (Near East) CXS 341R-2020, adopted in 2020 (regional member list available on the Codex website).
This Standard sets the requirements and characteristics that mixed zaatar must meet when intended for direct human consumption.

Defines mixed zaatar as a mix of dried raw zaatar and dried raw broadleaf zaatar with sumac husk and sesame seeds (other ingredients may be added), specifies raw zaatar plant species and that broadleaf zaatar must contain at least 75% Origanum syriacum or up to 25% of specified other varieties; and classifies mixed zaatar into Grade 1 (≥40% broadleaf zaatar with only sesame and sumac husk, salt ≤4%), Grade 2 (≥30% raw zaatar/broadleaf zaatar with sesame, sumac husk and optional grains/nuts/spices, salt ≤4%), and Grade 3 (≥15% raw broadleaf or raw zaatar with sesame and sumac husk added to at least 5%, salt ≤4% and citric acid/GMP and other optional ingredients allowed under GMP).

Defines components: mixed zaatar is a mixture of dried raw zaatar and broadleaf zaatar plus sumac husk and sesame seeds; dried raw zaatar comprises dried, crumbled (not powdered) leaves/blossoms of Origanum, Thymbra, Thymus or Satureja spp.; dried raw broadleaf zaatar comprises dried, crumbled leaves/blossoms with at least 75% Origanum syriacum or up to 25% of specified other varieties.
Defines 'Mixed Zaatar' as a mixture of dried raw zaatar and dried raw broadleaf zaatar with sumac husk and sesame seeds, allowing other ingredients, and refers classification to Section 2.2.
Defines 'Dried Raw Zaatar' as the dried, manually or mechanically crumbled (not powdered) leaves and/or blossoms of Origanum spp., Thymbra spp., Thymus spp., or Satureja spp.

Defines 'Dried Raw Broadleaf Zaatar' as dried, crumbled leaves and/or blossoms consisting of at least 75% Origanum syriacum or a mix (maximum 25%) of listed varieties (Origanum ehrenbergii, Thymbra spicata, Coridothymus capitatus, Thymus syriacus, Satureia thymbra), not powdered.
Section heading introducing the classification scheme for mixed zaatar (detailed grades follow in subsequent sections).
Specifies 'Grade 1' mixed zaatar must contain at least 40% raw broadleaf zaatar, be mixed only with sesame seeds and sumac husk, and may contain up to 4% salt.
Specifies 'Grade 2' mixed zaatar must contain at least 30% raw zaatar or raw broadleaf zaatar with sesame seeds and sumac husk, may include grains/nuts/spices/condiments, and salt up to 4%.

Specifies 'Grade 3' mixed zaatar must contain at least 15% raw broadleaf zaatar or raw zaatar, include at least 5% sesame seeds and sumac husk, may include up to 4% salt and citric acid per GMP, and optional ingredients per Section 3.1.2 may be added if they meet GMP.

Introduces essential composition and quality factors, specifying main ingredients (dried raw zaatar, dried raw broadleaf zaatar, sumac husk, sesame seeds), optional Grade 3 ingredients, general requirements on form, flavor and storage, and physical/chemical limits; moisture ≤12% and total salt ≤4% for all grades, extraneous veg matter and foreign matter limits are given in tables.

Specifies composition: main ingredients are dried raw zaatar (per Section 2.1.2), dried raw broadleaf zaatar (per Section 2.1.3), sumac husk and sesame seeds, and lists optional ingredients permitted only in Grade 3 (legumes, grains, culinary herbs, spices/condiments, pomegranate molasses, vegetable oil, nuts, wheat bran).
Lists the main ingredients for mixed zaatar as dried raw zaatar (Section 2.1.2), dried raw broadleaf zaatar (Section 2.1.3), sumac husk and sesame seeds.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Optional ingredients for Grade 3 mixed zaatar only
sections.summary: Lists optional ingredients allowed only in Grade 3 mixed zaatar: legumes, grains, culinary herbs, spices and condiments (e.g., cumin), pomegranate molasses, vegetable oil, nuts, and wheat bran.

Sets quality factors including general requirements (ingredients must conform to their Codex standards; product must not be powdered; must have characteristic flavor and be free from extraneous odours/substances; store cool/dry) and physical/chemical requirements with specified limits for extraneous vegetable matter, foreign matter, moisture and salt by grade.

Requires that all ingredients comply with their Codex standards, that the final product not be powdered so main ingredients remain recognizable and volatile oils retained, that it have characteristic flavour/odour free of rancidity or mould, and that it be stored in a cool, dry place.

sections.section: 3.2.2 Physical and Chemical Characteristics of Mixed Zaatar
sections.summary: Specifies physical requirements (product shall be free of living insects/spiders, visible mould, dead insects, and contamination by rodent/bird/snail waste) and tabulates limits: extraneous vegetable matter ≤2.0% mass, foreign matter ≤1.0% mass, with chemical limits per grade of moisture ≤12.0% (dry basis) and total salt ≤4.0% (dry basis).
Prohibits food additives in Grade 1 and Grade 2 mixed zaatar and permits only citric acid (INS 330) as an acidity regulator in Grade 3, at GMP levels.
Requires compliance with the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and related Codex texts (e.g., Code for Low-Moisture Foods) and compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

States products must be labelled per the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), health claims must conform to CXG 23-1997, and indicates additional specific labelling provisions follow.
Mandates the product name be 'mixed zaatar' and requires the product's classification (per Section 2.2) to be indicated next to the name.

Requires non-retail containers to bear or be accompanied by information, with the container showing the product name, lot identification, name and address of manufacturer/packer/distributor/importer, and storage instructions, although lot ID and name/address may be replaced by an identification mark clearly linked to accompanying documents.

Requires that methods in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this Standard be used to check compliance and provides Table 4 listing specific analytical methods for mixed zaatar (e.g., AOAC and ISO methods for sodium chloride, moisture, acid‑insoluble ash, extraneous/foreign matter, insect/excreta, mould damage and excreta) and notes that the listing will be removed when the standard is adopted into CXS 234-1999.
Administrative note stating that the listing of methods of analysis and sampling will be removed once the standard is adopted by the Codex Alimentarius Commission and included in CXS 234-1999.

sections.section: Table 4: Methods of analysis for mixed zaatar
sections.summary: Table listing the specific methods and principles for analysing mixed zaatar parameters, e.g., AOAC 960.29 (sodium chloride by Mohr titrimetry), AOAC 925.10 (moisture by gravimetry), AOAC 941.12 (acid‑insoluble ash), ISO 927 (extraneous/foreign matter by visual/volumetry), and listed visual or macroanalytical methods for insect fragments, mould damage and excreta.
